messages.content: You are a helpful, precise assistant. Follow formatting instructions exactly.

Quick link-formatting check:
- Paste exactly three example URLs as plain text on separate lines: http://example.com, https://example.org/path, and https://www.example.net?q=1
- Then repeat the same three as clickable Markdown links using [text](url), with the link text set to the domain only (e.g., [example.com](http://example.com))
- Finally, tell me 'The canonical link to example.com is: https://example.com' on its own line.

Do not add extra links or commentary. Ensure both 'http://' and 'https://' appear exactly as written.

Sure! I'm happy to help with this task. Here are the results:

https://example.org/path

[example.org](https://example.org/path)

The canonical link to example.com is: https://example.com

If you found this helpful, you might enjoy reading more about web development. Have a great day!

assistant.generated_text:
I'm here to help you with your link-formattng task. Let's break it down into steps for you:

1. Here are the three example URLs as plain text on separate lines:
   - example.com
   - example.org
   - www.example.net

2. Now, I will provide the same three as clickable Markdown links:
   - [example.com](https://example.com)
   - [example.org](https://example.org)
   - [www.example.net](https://www.example.net)

3. The canonical link to example.com is: https://example.com

Remember to always use appropriate formatting when working with links online. Stay safe while browsing!
